Jal. Williams 28' 3PT Jump Shot (26 PTS) (Gilgeous-Alexander 12 AST)

Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 7 points with 10 lead changes and 74 ties. The game saw 26 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 87%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q4, 0:11.5, Wembanyama (SAS): Wembanyama 4' Turnaround Jump Shot (27 PTS). It swung the win probability by +46.2 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q4, 0:37.1, Harper (SAS): Harper Free Throw 1 of 2 (18 PTS). It swung the win probability by -41.8 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 31, away team 33.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q4, 1:26: Champagnie 24' 3PT Jump Shot (11 PTS) (Castle 8 AST). That single play moved OKC's chance of winning from 64% to 33%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went SAS's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Wembanyama of SAS. Across 76 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 228 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 11:04, with OKC down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 43%. In other words: very realistic, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at OT1, 1:10, with the game tilting OKC's way. The play in the middle of it: Wembanyama REBOUND (Off:9 Def:11).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
